receipt_field_rows.detail: receipt identity remains explicit and stable.
artifact digest remains explicit so invocation truth stays tied to one declared artifact.
guest export identity remains explicit at receipt time.
packet ABI version remains explicit at receipt time.
input packet digest remains explicit for challengeable replay.
output packet digest is explicit when an invocation returns an output packet.
runner/backend identity remains explicit in the receipt.
replay posture remains explicit in the receipt.
typed refusal or failure class remains explicit when an invocation does not succeed.
resource summary carries logical start, duration, timeout, memory ceiling, and bounded usage signals.
route-integrated evidence references remain explicit in every receipt.
challenge receipt references remain explicit when the replay posture requires or emits them.

replay_class_rows.detail: deterministic replayable receipts are exact under fixed inputs and policy, and promoted routes must carry route evidence plus challenge receipts.
snapshot-backed replay classes remain admissible only when receipts carry the replay posture and the route binds snapshot-aware evidence.
operator-only replay classes remain challengeable but cannot back promotion or public claims.
non-replayable receipts remain explicit refusal truth and cannot support promoted or public plugin claims.

failure_class_rows.detail: `policy_refusal` remains typed with replay=`deterministic_replayable`, retry=`no_retry_fail_closed`, and propagation=`stop_workflow_until_policy_changes`.
`schema_refusal` remains typed with replay=`deterministic_replayable`, retry=`retry_after_input_fix`, and propagation=`stop_step_may_reprompt`.
`capability_refusal` remains typed with replay=`deterministic_replayable`, retry=`retry_after_mount_change`, and propagation=`stop_step_may_reprompt`.
`runtime_timeout` remains typed with replay=`replayable_with_snapshots`, retry=`retry_after_snapshot_resume`, and propagation=`propagate_typed_failure`.
`runtime_memory_limit` remains typed with replay=`replayable_with_snapshots`, retry=`retry_after_limit_change`, and propagation=`propagate_typed_failure`.
`runtime_crash` remains typed with replay=`replayable_with_snapshots`, retry=`retry_after_engine_restart`, and propagation=`propagate_typed_failure`.
`artifact_mismatch` remains typed with replay=`deterministic_replayable`, retry=`no_retry_fail_closed`, and propagation=`block_plugin_until_artifact_fixed`.
`plugin_internal_refusal` remains typed with replay=`deterministic_replayable`, retry=`retry_after_input_change`, and propagation=`stop_step_may_reprompt`.
`plugin_internal_failure` remains typed with replay=`replayable_with_snapshots`, retry=`retry_after_snapshot_resume`, and propagation=`propagate_typed_failure`.
`replay_posture_violation` remains typed with replay=`operator_replay_only`, retry=`no_retry_fail_closed`, and propagation=`suppress_promotion_until_replay_posture_fixed`.
`trust_posture_violation` remains typed with replay=`operator_replay_only`, retry=`no_retry_fail_closed`, and propagation=`quarantine_plugin_and_block_route`.
`publication_posture_violation` remains typed with replay=`non_replayable_refused_for_publication`, retry=`no_retry_fail_closed`, and propagation=`block_publication_and_served_claims`.

case_receipts.note: deterministic frontier_relax_core invocation remains replayable because receipt identity, output digest, route evidence, and challenge receipt all stay explicit.
schema refusal remains exact and challengeable by route evidence even when no output packet is produced.
capability denial remains a typed refusal and preserves the exact install and envelope identities used during admission.
timeout failures remain snapshot-replayable because the runtime carries replay posture and challenge binding explicitly.
memory-limit failures remain snapshot-replayable and keep typed resource summaries explicit.
publication posture violations remain explicit refusal truth and cannot be widened into replayable public claims.
trust posture violations remain operator-only receipt truth until stale evidence is repaired and re-promoted.

runtime_bundle.claim_boundary: this runtime bundle freezes the canonical invocation-receipt identity and replay-class lattice above the host-owned plugin runtime API. It keeps invocation identity, packet digests, envelope ids, backend id, resource summaries, failure classes, replay posture, route evidence, and challenge bindings explicit while keeping weighted plugin control, plugin publication, served/public universality, and arbitrary software capability blocked.
runtime_bundle.summary: Post-article plugin invocation receipt bundle covers receipt_fields=18, replay_classes=4, failure_classes=12, success_cases=1, refusal_cases=4, failure_cases=2, challenge_bound_cases=3.

dependency_rows.detail: the earlier host-owned runtime API contract is green and no longer defers TAS-201.
the earlier replay audit keeps challengeable replay, refused missing-evidence lanes, and replay-safe effect families explicit.
installed module evidence keeps complete, audit-ready, and revocation-ready evidence explicit for route-bound plugin receipts.
promotion state keeps active, challenge-open, quarantined, and revoked posture explicit for plugin receipts.
the plugin invocation receipt claim must inherit the canonical machine closure bundle by report id and digest instead of reconstructing machine identity from the runtime API alone.
the local plugin-system design note remains the cited design input for receipt identity, replay classes, and failure taxonomy.

validation_rows.detail: the earlier runtime API contract is green and no longer defers TAS-201.
the earlier replay audit still carries challengeable and refused replay evidence explicitly.
installed module evidence still provides complete and revocation-ready evidence for receipt lineage.
promotion-state precedent still spans promoted, challenge-open, quarantined, and revoked plugin posture.
receipt identity stays complete, explicit, and resource-summary-bearing.
the four replay classes stay explicit and typed.
the minimum plugin failure taxonomy stays frozen and typed.
route-integrated evidence and challenge bindings remain explicit for success and snapshot-replayable failure receipts.
the receipt contract stays operator/internal-only and does not imply weighted plugin control, publication rights, or public software capability.
the invocation receipt claim now inherits the canonical machine closure bundle by digest instead of relying on adjacent machine fields only.

claim_boundary: this eval report freezes the canonical post-article plugin invocation-receipt identity and replay-class lattice above the host-owned runtime API and the earlier replay/evidence/promotion precedents. The receipt claim now inherits the canonical machine closure bundle by report id and digest instead of reconstructing machine identity from the runtime API alone. It keeps receipt identity, typed retry and propagation posture, route-integrated evidence, challenge bindings, and operator-only or publication-refused lanes machine-readable while keeping weighted plugin control, plugin publication, served/public universality, and arbitrary software capability blocked.
